questions.text: The information to be communicated in a data communications system is the ________.
questions.explanation: In a data communications system, the **Message** is the information or data to be communicated. Common forms of information include text, numbers, pictures, audio, and video.

questions.text: ________ refers to the physical or logical arrangement of a network.
questions.explanation: The **Topology** of a network is the geometric representation of the relationship of all the links and linking devices (usually called nodes) to one another.

questions.text: ________ is measured by frequency of failure.
questions.explanation: Network **Reliability** is measured by the frequency of failure, the time it takes a link to recover from a failure, and the network's robustness in a catastrophe.

questions.text: The standards that have been legislated by an officially recognized body are ________ standards.
questions.explanation: **De jure** standards (meaning "by law" or "by regulation") are those that have been legislated by an officially recognized body. De facto standards are those that have been widely adopted but not officially sanctioned.

questions.text: There are ________ categories for unshielded twisted pair cables.
questions.explanation: The EIA/TIA (Electronic Industries Association/Telecommunications Industry Association) defines **seven** categories of unshielded twisted-pair (UTP) cable, designated as Category 1 through Category 7, each suited for different transmission speeds and applications.

questions.text: A ________ connection is one in which more than two specific devices share a single link.
questions.explanation: A **Multipoint** (also called multidrop) connection is one in which more than two specific devices share a single link. In a multipoint environment, the capacity of the channel is shared, either spatially or temporally.

questions.text: In the ________ method, the time is divided into intervals.
questions.explanation: In the **Reservation** access method, time is divided into intervals. In each interval, a reservation frame precedes the data frames sent in that interval. If there are \( N \) stations, there are \( N \) reservation minislots in the reservation frame.

questions.text: ________ was the first network.
questions.explanation: The **ARPANET** (Advanced Research Projects Agency Network), developed by the U.S. Department of Defense in the late 1960s, was the world's first operational packet-switching network and the predecessor of the global Internet.

questions.text: The layer that is responsible for error control is \_\_\_\_\_\_.
questions.explanation: Error control is implemented in both the Data Link layer (for node-to-node delivery) and the Transport layer (for end-to-end delivery of the entire message).

questions.text: Network \_\_\_\_\_\_ can be measured in many ways, including transit time and response time.
questions.explanation: Network performance is often evaluated based on transit time (the time for a message to travel from one device to another) and response time (the elapsed time between an inquiry and a response).

questions.text: Propagation method where signals are transmitted in straight lines directly from antenna is called \_\_\_\_\_\_.
questions.explanation: Line-of-sight propagation refers to the transmission of signals in a straight line from antenna to antenna.

questions.text: In a datagram network, resources are allocated \_\_\_\_\_\_.
questions.explanation: A datagram network is connectionless; there is no setup or teardown phase. Resources like bandwidth and buffers are allocated on demand as packets arrive.

questions.text: \_\_\_\_\_\_ is delivering data as they are produced, in the same order, and without significant delay.
questions.explanation: Real-time transmission refers to the delivery of data as it is produced, in its original order, and without significant delay (jitter).

questions.text: Determining the data rate is the responsibility of the \_\_\_\_\_\_ layer.
questions.explanation: The Physical layer handles the bit rate (data rate), defining the number of bits sent each second.

questions.text: Without a \_\_\_\_\_\_, two devices may be connected but not communicating.
questions.explanation: A protocol is a set of rules that governs data communications. Without a common protocol, two devices cannot understand each other even if they are physically connected.

questions.text: Which organization has authority over interstate and international commerce in communications?
questions.explanation: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is a regulatory agency in the United States that has authority over interstate and international commerce in the field of communications.

questions.text: Message can be \_\_\_\_\_\_.
questions.explanation: In data communications, a message can take several forms, including text, numbers, images, audio, and video.

questions.text: The vulnerable time for CSMA is the \_\_\_\_\_\_.
questions.explanation: In CSMA, the vulnerable time is the time it takes for a signal to propagate from one end of the medium to the other (Propagation time, \(T_p\)). If another station starts transmitting within this window, a collision occurs.

questions.text: \_\_\_\_\_\_ is a working document with no official status and a 6-month lifetime.
questions.explanation: An Internet draft is a working document with no official status and a lifetime of six months. It may eventually become an RFC (Request for Comments).

questions.text: The address space of IPv4 is \_\_\_\_\_\_.
questions.explanation: IPv4 uses 32-bit addresses, so the total address space is \( 2^{32} \), which is approximately 4.3 billion addresses.

questions.text: The layer that adds a trailer is \_\_\_\_\_\_.
questions.explanation: The Data Link layer is unique in the OSI model because it adds both a header and a trailer to the data unit (packet) it receives from the network layer to create a frame.

questions.text: Jitter is variation in the packet _______ time.
questions.explanation: Jitter is defined as the variation in the delay of received packets, specifically the variation in packet arrival times.

questions.text: The layer that adds the logical address is _______.
questions.explanation: The Network layer (Layer 3 of the OSI model) is responsible for logical addressing (e.g., IP addresses) and routing of packets.

questions.text: The TCP/IP host-to-network layer is equivalent to the _______.
questions.explanation: The Host-to-network layer (also known as the Network Access layer) in the TCP/IP model encompasses the functions of both the Physical and Data Link layers of the OSI model.

questions.text: The layer responsible for translation, compression, and encryption is the _______.
questions.explanation: The Presentation layer (Layer 6 of the OSI model) handles the formatting and syntax of data, including translation, compression, and encryption.

questions.text: The protocol used in the transport layer in the TCP/IP model is _______.
questions.explanation: While both TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) and UDP (User Datagram Protocol) operate at the transport layer, TCP is the primary connection-oriented protocol for which the model is named.

questions.text: The data link layer ensures _______.
questions.explanation: The Data Link layer provides node-to-node delivery and implements mechanisms (like framing and CRC) to ensure error-free transmission over a physical link.

questions.text: In coaxial cable, the _______ can be made of metal foil.
questions.explanation: The outer conductor of a coaxial cable, which acts as a shield against electromagnetic interference, is often made of metal foil or braided wire.

questions.text: There are _______ categories for unshielded twisted pair cables.
questions.explanation: The TIA/EIA standard traditionally recognizes seven main categories of UTP cable (Cat 1 to Cat 7) for various data transmission speeds and bandwidths.

questions.text: In fiber-optic cable, the signal is propagated along the inner core by _______.
questions.explanation: Fiber-optic cables use the principle of total internal reflection to bounce light signals along the core without significant loss.

questions.text: Which of the following can be an Ethernet physical address?

choices.label: D
choices.text: None of the choices are correct

questions.explanation: An Ethernet MAC address must be 48 bits (6 bytes) long, represented as 6 groups of 2 hexadecimal digits. Option C has exactly 6 pairs: 07, 01, 02, 01, 2C, and 4B.

questions.text: In a _______ network, resources are allocated on demand.
questions.explanation: In a datagram (packet-switched) network, no dedicated resources are reserved; instead, resources are used as needed (on demand) on a per-packet basis.

questions.text: The _______ approach is used in the Internet.
questions.explanation: The Internet primarily uses the datagram approach at the network layer (IP), where each packet is treated independently and routed based on its destination address.
